records.ety: [L. inculcatus, p. p. of inculcare to tread on; pref. in- in, on + calcare to tread, fr. calx the heel; perh. akin to E. heel. Cf. 2d Calk, Heel.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Inculcated; p. pr. & vb. n. Inculcating (?).]

defs: To teach and impress by frequent repetitions or admonitions; to urge on the mind; as, Christ inculcates on his followers humility.
quotes: The most obvious and necessary duties of life they have not yet had authority enough to enforce and inculcate upon men's minds.
